info.title: Blueticks API
info.description: Public API for Blueticks WhatsApp automation.

to.description: Recipient. Either +E164 phone (e.g. +14155551234) or a WhatsApp JID (e.g. 14155551234@c.us). Stored as +E164.

variables.description:
Per-contact substitution values used by campaign templates.
Each key becomes a token usable as `{key}` in a campaign `text`. Lookup is case-insensitive (e.g. `{firstName}` resolves `firstname`).
Built-in tokens always available regardless of variables: `{whatsappname}`, `{displayname}`, `{phone}`, `{mobile}`. Setting a variable with the same name overrides the built-in.
Storage: keys are persisted as text-cell column titles on the audience board, so values flow back via audiences.get under the same names.
Limits: at most 32 keys per contact; values up to 1024 chars; keys must match /^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]{0,31}$/.

audience_id.description: ID of the audience to target. Each audience contact contributes one outgoing message.

text.description:
Message body. Supports per-contact substitution using single-brace tokens like `{firstName}` or `{product}`.
Resolution rules (case-insensitive, whitespace stripped):
  - Built-ins always available: `{whatsappname}` (WA profile name), `{displayname}` (firstname+lastname fallback), `{phone}` / `{mobile}`.
  - Custom variables: any property attached to a contact via audiences.append_contacts becomes a token of the same name.
  - Unresolved tokens are left as literal `{token}` in the sent message.
Templates use SINGLE braces `{var}` — `{{var}}` is treated as literal text.

media_url.description: Public https URL of an image/video/document to attach.

media_caption.description: Caption for the media attachment. Requires media_url.

from.description: Sender phone in +E164. When omitted, the workspace default sender is used.

on_missing_variable.description:
Behavior when a template references a variable that is not present on a contact:
  - `fail` (default): reject the request at create time; no messages are sent. Returns 400 with `data.examples` listing offending contacts.
  - `skip`: accept the campaign; contacts missing variables receive the message with literal `{token}` left in place.

original_quality.description: Set to false when the bytes returned are NOT the original sender uploaded — i.e. WA released the original from memory and we synthesized from a smaller WA-generated preview (#113). Currently only happens for own-sent newsletter media (messages you sent to a @newsletter chat). When absent or true, the bytes are the genuine original. Consumers can use this to warn users / decide whether to retry from a different source.

media_unavailable.description: Set when the bytes could not be downloaded or never existed. "expired" = WA aged the file out of CDN retention (typically older than ~30 days for documents) and there is nothing to retry. "fetching" = WA is mid-download from its CDN — transient, the server already retried for ~10s; calling again in a few seconds usually resolves to bytes. "awaiting_sender" = WA's CDN does not have the file but has asked the sender's device to reupload it. The reupload only completes when the sender's WhatsApp client is open and online; closed/backgrounded apps block it indefinitely. Tell the user to ask the sender to open WhatsApp; do not poll. "error" = unexpected failure; "no_media" = the message is text-only / revoked / location / vcard, i.e. it never carried media — caller should not have invoked this tool on this messageKey. Absent when bytes were not needed or when the fetch succeeded.

timezone.description: IANA timezone (e.g. "America/New_York"), or null if unset.
IANA timezone (e.g. "America/New_York"), or null if unset.

get.description: Retrieve the account the API key belongs to.
List messages sent through the API, newest first (cursor-paginated).

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Optional key for safe retries. Identical bodies with the same key return the original response; differing bodies return 409.

post.description: Rotate the signing secret. Returns the new secret exactly once. Requires `webhooks:write` scope.
Append contacts to an audience. Duplicates (by `to`) are skipped. Requires `audiences:write`.

to.description: Recipient. Either +E164 phone (e.g. +14155551234) or a WhatsApp JID (e.g. 14155551234@c.us). Stored as +E164.

patch.description: Edit a contact`s phone or variables. Requires `audiences:write`.

delete.description: Remove a contact from an audience. Requires `audiences:write`.

post.description: Pause a running campaign. 409 if not `running`. Requires `campaigns:write`.
Resume a paused campaign. 409 if not `paused`. Requires `campaigns:write`.
Cancel a campaign. 409 if already terminal. Requires `campaigns:write`.

get.description: Cursor-paginated list of messages in a chat. Supports free-text search (`query`), date range (`since`/`until`), and message-kind filtering (`message_types`). Requires `chats:read`.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Free-text search across message bodies and captions.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Pagination cursor from a previous response.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: Lower bound (ISO 8601). Only messages with timestamp >= since.

parameters.name: until
parameters.description: Upper bound (ISO 8601). Only messages with timestamp <= until.

parameters.name: message_types
parameters.description: Filter to specific message kinds. Comma-separated or repeated. Useful for "all PDFs" (`document`) or "images today" (`image`). System events (gp2/revoked/newsletter_notification) are excluded by default unless explicitly listed here.

get.description: Fetch a single message by its WhatsApp message key. Requires `chats:read`.

Download the media attached to a WhatsApp message (image, video, document, audio).
Returns either a hosted URL (`url`) or inline `data_base64`, plus mimetype + filename.

CAVEAT: for own-sent newsletter media (messages you sent to a `@newsletter` chat), the bytes returned are a WA-generated preview JPEG (~7KB) rather than the original you uploaded — WA releases the original blob from memory immediately after the unencrypted upload. The response will include `original_quality:false` when this fallback is in effect; the caller can warn the user or decide to retry from a different source.

Requires `chats:read`.

original_quality.description: Set to false when the bytes returned are NOT the original sender uploaded — i.e. WA released the original from memory and we synthesized from a smaller WA-generated preview (#113). Currently only happens for own-sent newsletter media (messages you sent to a @newsletter chat). When absent or true, the bytes are the genuine original. Consumers can use this to warn users / decide whether to retry from a different source.

media_unavailable.description: Set when the bytes could not be downloaded or never existed. "expired" = WA aged the file out of CDN retention (typically older than ~30 days for documents) and there is nothing to retry. "fetching" = WA is mid-download from its CDN — transient, the server already retried for ~10s; calling again in a few seconds usually resolves to bytes. "awaiting_sender" = WA's CDN does not have the file but has asked the sender's device to reupload it. The reupload only completes when the sender's WhatsApp client is open and online; closed/backgrounded apps block it indefinitely. Tell the user to ask the sender to open WhatsApp; do not poll. "error" = unexpected failure; "no_media" = the message is text-only / revoked / location / vcard, i.e. it never carried media — caller should not have invoked this tool on this messageKey. Absent when bytes were not needed or when the fetch succeeded.

tags.name: Account
tags.description: Account metadata for the authenticated API key.

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description: Register URLs to receive signed HTTPS POSTs on Blueticks events.

tags.name: Audiences
tags.description: Reusable contact lists used as targets for messages and campaigns.

tags.name: Campaigns
tags.description: Schedule audiences for paced bulk delivery with pause/resume/cancel controls.

tags.name: Chats
tags.description: Read WhatsApp chats and their messages on the connected engine.

tags.name: Groups
tags.description: Create and manage WhatsApp groups via the connected engine.

tags.name: Contacts
tags.description: Read WhatsApp contacts known to the connected engine.

tags.name: Engines
tags.description: Inspect or control the WhatsApp engine connected to the workspace.

tags.name: ScheduledMessages
tags.description: List, retrieve, update, and cancel previously-scheduled WhatsApp messages.
